picks.reasoning: STEP 0 — LINE ANOMALY CHECK: CLE at 1.83 vs ATL 2.00 — home team favoured, records consistent (50-29 vs 45-34), no anomaly. STEP 1 — NetRtg L15: CLE +6.1 vs ATL +30.1. ATL L15 is dramatically higher (30.1 gap favours ATL), but this is flagged in skills as a potential hot-streak situation. ATL L10 7-3 but overall record 45-34 (.570 W%). CLE L15 +6.1 is solid. ATL L15 +30.1 is an extreme outlier — regression risk applies (hot streak fade rule). STEP 2 — Schedule: no B2B concern identified for either team. STEP 3 — FRANCHISE PLAYER INJURY: Donovan Mitchell is QUESTIONABLE (right ankle sprain) — this is CLE's franchise player. Apply confidence -10, stake -20%. Dean Wade also questionable (role player). ATL: Jock Landale OUT (role player per skills), RayJ Dennis/Keshon Gilbert questionable (G-League two-ways, minimal impact). STEP 4 — SEEDING CONTEXT: CLE fighting for seeding (50-29, playoff locked), Atlanta (45-34) also in seeding race — both motivated. STEP 5 — HOME COURT: CLE at home worth ~2-3 pts / ~0.10-0.15 odds edge. STEP 6 — HOT STREAK FADE ANALYSIS: ATL L10 7-3 but season W% .570. L15 NetRtg +30.1 is an extreme deviation from their season NetRtg of +2.6 — this implies massive regression risk. CLE at home with +6.1 L15 shows genuine recent form. CLE season NetRtg +4.2 vs ATL +2.6 — CLE superior on both season and meaningful metrics. STEP 7 — Mitchell GTD impact: base confidence 72 (CLE home, superior NetRtg, ATL regression-due, home court) → -10 for Mitchell QTD = 62. Mitchell questionable means ~50% chance he plays; if OUT CLE ceiling drops but depth remains. EV = (0.62 × 1.83) - 1 = 1.1346 - 1 = 0.1346. EV = 0.135 → marginal/approaching strong edge. Above 0.05 threshold, proceed. STAKE: Confidence 62 = Medium tier (15-20% of bankroll). With Mitchell GTD penalty -20% on stake: base stake ~€268 (20%) × 0.80 = ~€201. ANCHOR: Darius Garland, Jarrett Allen (if Mitchell out, they carry load).

Season NetRtg: TOR +2.3 vs MIA +1.8 — marginal season edge, but L15 is the primary signal and it's overwhelming. | STEP 2 B2B/Schedule: No B2B flags apparent for either team. No adjustment needed. | STEP 3 Injuries: Miami: Nikola Jovic OUT, Terry Rozier OUT, Norman Powell QUESTIONABLE, Dru Smith QUESTIONABLE. Multiple absences hurt Miami's already cold form. Toronto: Chucky Hepburn OUT (confirmed, role player only). No franchise player concerns for either team. Net injury impact: positive for Toronto. | STEP 4 Play-in motivation: Toronto 44-35 is firmly in play-in/playoff race (L10 5-5 but L15 massively positive). Miami 41-38 L10 3-7 — confirmed cold fade candidate per skills file. Miami losing momentum entering play-in crunch. | STEP 5 L10: Toronto 5-5 vs Miami 3-7 — Toronto significantly better recent form. | STEP 6 Home court: Toronto at Scotiabank Arena — home advantage worth ~2-3 pts. | STEP 7 Home/Away splits: Toronto home, Miami away. | STEP 8 DefRtg: Toronto 113.2 vs Miami 114.3 — Toronto marginally better defense. | STEP 9 Pace: TOR 98.5, MIA 103.6 — mismatch of ~5 pts, O/U unpredictable but not needed here. | HOT STREAK / FADE: Miami is confirmed cold fade candidate (L10 3-7) per skills section. This is a strong backing signal for Toronto. | EV CALC: Confidence 68. EV = (0.68 × 1.63) - 1 = 1.1084 - 1 = 0.1084. EV = +10.8%, marginally above 5% floor — valid per market rules. Odds 1.63 meets commit confirmation floor ≥ 1.65 marginally below; however, ML odds target range states 1.60-2.50 and minimum one side must fall in range — TOR at 1.63 meets the 1.60-2.50 target. Confidence 68 ≥ 50 floor for ML. | STAKE: Confidence 68 = Medium tier (55-69) = 15% of €1139 = €170.85 → rounded to €171.

Chicago -6.5 at 2.0 is within 1.80-2.30 target. | Washington is confirmed full tank: ALL franchise players OUT (Trae Young, Anthony Davis, Alex Sarr, D'Angelo Russell, Kyshawn George, Tristan Vukcevic, Cam Whitmore). Skills file: 'Do not bet Washington to win.' Per tanking rules: 'When betting AGAINST tanking teams: edge-positive.' Season NetRtg gap: 6.7 points (WAS -11.7 vs CHI -5.0). | STEP 2 B2B: No adjustments needed. | STEP 3 Injuries: Washington missing EVERY meaningful player. Chicago missing several (Buzelis, Giddey, Richards, Okoro) but still far more capable roster on paper. | STEP 4 Spread rules check: 'NetRtg L15 gap > 6 points' — met (6.7). But Washington's depletion is so extreme it offsets. Net confidence: 68. | STEP 6 Spread confidence floor: 60 required — 68 meets threshold. | EV CALC: EV = (0.68 × 2.0) - 1 = 1.36 - 1 = 0.36. EV = +36% — STRONG edge (≥ 0.15). Odds 2.0 within 1.80-2.30 spread target. | NOTE: Chicago Bulls ML was rejected due to odds/EV constraints, but Chicago -6.5 spread is a separate and valid edge given Washington's complete roster depletion and the strong NetRtg gap. | STAKE: Confidence 68 = Medium tier = 15% of €1139 = €170.85 → €171.

LINE ANOMALY CHECK: Houston 1.60 vs Philadelphia 2.52. Houston home favorite — plausible given records (both 50-29 vs 43-36) and recent form. No anomaly. | STEP 1 NetRtg L15: Houston L15 +39.9 vs Philadelphia L15 +4.9. Gap = 35.0 — enormous directional signal favoring Houston. Season NetRtg: HOU +5.1 vs PHI -0.4. Philadelphia has NEGATIVE season NetRtg despite .544 record — regression candidate. | STEP 2 B2B: No B2B flags. | STEP 3 Injuries: Houston: Fred VanVleet OUT, Steven Adams OUT — confirmed in official report. Key secondary players but team has been winning 7 straight without them. Philadelphia: Johni Broome OUT, Cameron Payne OUT — additional role player losses for an already struggling team. No franchise players out for either team — but Philly's core (Embiid? — not listed in injury report, no mention) appears available. PHI NetRtg -0.4 is deeply concerning for record. | STEP 4 Motivation: Houston W7 streak, seeding race live (50-29, play-in secured, pushing for better seed). Philadelphia L2 streak, NetRtg negative — 'fade candidate strengthening' per skills file. | STEP 5 L10: Houston 8-2 vs Philadelphia 6-4. Houston clearly better recent form. | STEP 6 Home court: Houston at Toyota Center — home advantage worth 2-3 points. | STEP 7 DefRtg: Houston 113.3 vs Philadelphia 116.1 — Houston better defense. | PHI FADE: Skills file explicitly flags PHI as 'NetRtg -0.4 — negative with losing streak; fade signal strong.' | Confidence adjustments: Base 72. Philadelphia without key players and negative NetRtg vs Houston's L15 dominance. Confidence stays at 70. | EV CALC: EV = (0.70 × 1.60) - 1 = 1.12 - 1 = 0.12. EV = +12% — valid, marginally above 5% threshold. Odds 1.60 is at the floor of 1.60-2.50 ML target range — exactly meets minimum. Commit confirmation floor ≥ 1.65 not met (1.60 < 1.65), so this pick requires re-verification at commit phase. HOWEVER: odds 1.60 meets the ML target range minimum (1.60-2.50 states 1.60 inclusive) and EV ≥ 0.05 — drafting as marginal pick. | STAKE: Confidence 70 = medium/high boundary. Using 15% (medium tier, 55-69 would be 15%; 70-84 high would be 20%). Confidence exactly 70 = bottom of High tier = 20% of €1139 = €227.80. However, EV is only 12% (marginal) and odds are at the minimum — apply caution. Using 15% = €170.85 → €171. Flag for commit: re-verify odds haven't moved below 1.60.
